cards.back: A representation of an algorithm where every operation uses a source operand, a destination operand, and an operand

cards.front: What is the main advantage of Three Address Code?
cards.back: Simplifies optimization and code generation, as all instructions have a clear destination operand

cards.front: What is the purpose of the 'dummy' register in Three Address Code?
cards.back: To serve as a source operand for instructions that need one, and as a destination operand for instructions that need two

cards.front: What is the difference between a 'simple' Three Address Code and a 'full' Three Address Code?
cards.back: Simple Three Address Code uses only two registers, while full Three Address Code uses three registers

cards.front: What is the main challenge in implementing Three Address Code?
cards.back: Allocating sufficient registers to support the complexity of the Three Address Code
